name: Vantage Ghost
description: The Vantage Ghost, or 'Drishti Pret' in some local dialects, is a spectral entity in Indian folklore said to inhabit high places, particularly ancient watchtowers, crumbling forts, and remote mountain peaks. These ghosts are believed to be the lingering spirits of former guards, sentinels, or individuals who met a tragic end while on duty or seeking a strategic viewpoint. They are not inherently malevolent but are intensely territorial and protective of their vantage point. The Vantage Ghost manifests as a translucent, shimmering figure, often cloaked or armored in attire reminiscent of the era they belonged to. They possess an uncanny ability to observe everything within their domain, and their presence is often accompanied by chilling winds and a feeling of being watched. While they rarely directly harm humans, they are known to disorient travelers, create illusions of false paths, and instill overwhelming feelings of dread to deter intruders. Some believe these ghosts are bound to their post by a sense of duty, while others claim they are cursed to eternally guard a secret or protect a treasure. Their existence serves as a cautionary tale about the burdens of responsibility and the potential for tragic fates to bind souls to specific locations.
subtitle: Guardian spirits of high places
appearance: A translucent, shimmering figure resembling a former guard or soldier, often clad in outdated armor or simple robes. Their eyes glow faintly, and they exude an aura of cold air and ancient authority. They are often seen carrying a spectral weapon, such as a sword or spear.
habitat: Ancient watchtowers, crumbling forts, mountain peaks, and any location with a commanding view of the surrounding area.
behavior: Territorial and watchful. They observe all who enter their domain, and attempt to deter intruders through illusions, disorientation, and feelings of dread. Rarely directly aggressive, but fiercely protective of their vantage point.

keyMyths.title: The Lost Patrol
keyMyths.description: A group of soldiers vanished without a trace near an old fort, rumored to have been led astray by a Vantage Ghost protecting a hidden passage. The fort is now considered haunted and avoided by locals.

keyMyths.title: The Mountain's Watcher
keyMyths.description: A shepherd claimed to have been warned of an impending avalanche by a spectral figure on a mountain peak, saving his village. This story reinforces the belief that Vantage Ghosts can sometimes be benevolent protectors.

sources.author: Anjali Sharma
sources.work: Ghosts of the Raj: A Study of Colonial Hauntings
sources.description: Academic analysis of ghostly figures in historical locations.
sources.text: Academic analysis of ghostly figures in historical locations.

sources.author: Ramesh Patel
sources.description: Collection of traditional stories from the Western Ghats region.
sources.text: Collection of traditional stories from the Western Ghats region.
